{{Quote|Spast, Gunner, how did you survive your childhood?|[[Jae Akura]]|<ref name="Legacy 14" />}}
'''Gunn Yage''' was a [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gender/Legends|female]] [[Pilot/Legends|pilot]] in the [[Darth Krayt's Galactic Empire|Imperial]] [[Imperial Navy/Legends|Navy]] around [[137 ABY]]. Piloting ''Skull One''—a [[Predator-class fighter|''Predator''-class]] [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighter]]—under the [[Callsign/Legends|call sign]] of "'''Gunner'''", she led [[1st Imperial Center Core Defense Squadron|Skull Squadron]]. Yage was the daughter of [[Rulf Yage]] and [[Morrigan Corde|Nyna Calixte]], making her the maternal half-sister of [[Cade Skywalker]].

==Biography==
{{Quote|He was expecting a boy and instead he got me! And then my mother…|Gunn Yage on her [[Rulf Yage|father]] and [[Morrigan Corde|mother]]|<ref name="Legacy 14" />}}
[[File:GunnYage-Legacy2006-14.jpg|thumb|left|180px|Captain Yage chasing [[Cade Skywalker]] in a [[Predator-class fighter|''Predator''-class fighter]] over [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]].]]
Gunn Yage was the [[Yage family|daughter]] of [[Moff/Legends|Moffs]] [[Morrigan Corde|Nyna Calixte]] and [[Rulf Yage]], the latter of whom raised her like the son he wished he had. Growing up despising her mother for abandoning them to pursue a relationship with [[Grand Admiral/Legends|Grand Admiral]] [[Morlish Veed]], Gunn eventually gained her father's respect after climbing through the ranks with no preferential treatment. She served in the [[181st Imperial Fighter Wing/Legends|181st Imperial Fighter Wing]], eventually reaching the rank of [[Captain/Legends|captain]] and earning command of [[1st Imperial Center Core Defense Squadron|Skull Squadron]].

===Fleet Captain===
{{Quote|I've got it under control. My squad would fly into a meteor storm for me|Gunner to her father|<ref name="Legacy 15" />}}
During her time as leader of Skull [[Squadron/Legends|Squadron]], the other [[TIE pilot|pilots]] under her command matched her own exacting standards, and included [[Tev Rimon]], [[Jae Akura]], and [[Brodie Coburn]]. It was said that they would do anything for her. Around [[137 ABY]], Gunn led Skull Squadron in the pursuit of a vessel they believed to be [[Cade Skywalker]]'s ship, the [[Mynock (Helot-class)|''Mynock'']]'','' on [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]]. They were unable to catch him when Cade dove down into the undercity of the planet. Upon returning to their [[Skull Squadron Hangar|hangar]], Gunn was berated by her father for failing to capture him, and was told to meet him for dinner the next evening at a restaurant called the [[Naboo Queen (restaurant)|Naboo Queen]].

[[File:Gunnhateshermomlol.jpg|thumb|right|200px|Gunn Yage expresses her dislike of her mother.]]
While eating dinner with her father, Gunn's mother Nyna Calixte showed up. After calling her mother a [[Rancor/Legends|rancor]], she said she believed that the vessel she and her men engaged the day before was indeed the ''Mynock''. Shortly thereafter, Skull Squadron engaged the ''Mynock'' again, but this time the [[Wookiee/Legends|Wookiee]] [[Chak (Wookiee)|Chak]] was the pilot. When it seemed as though Gunn was about to disable the ship, she was informed by Captain [[Skolitz]] of Moff Yage's orders to disengage and abort the mission.

===Imperial Agent===
{{Quote|It's time for dear Gunner to step up her career and use her black-ops training.|Nyna Calixte about her daughter|<ref name="Legacy 37">[[Legacy (2006) 37|''Legacy'' (2006) 37]]</ref>}}
After destroying a stray [[Galactic Federation of Free Alliances|Galactic Alliance]] vessel, Gunn was contacted by her mother and given the mission to stop pirate attacks on Imperial shipments in the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim]], along with an agent of Calixte's named Morrigan Corde, to put her black ops training to its fullest. Upon arrival on [[Tatooine/Legends|Tatooine]], Yage met with [[Moff/Legends|Moff]] [[Nieve Gromia]], who hadn't heard from Corde in a many years. Gromia presented the latest intel regarding the pirates, which Gunn recognized their ship as the ''Mynock'', which belonged to Cade Skywalker, who was using his ancestor's name "[[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]]" as an alias. Gunn was sent to apprehend Cade. Unbeknownst to Gunn, Gromia contacted [[Black Sun/Legends|Black Sun]] [[Vigo/Legends|Vigo]] [[Lun Rask]], who sent a trio of [[Assassin/Legends|assassins]] to kill her and Cade before Corde arrived.

Gunn located Skywalker, who unbeknownst to her was her half-brother, at a [[Gusha's Luck|cantina]] in [[Mos Eisley/Legends|Mos Eisley]] and captured Cade by flirting with him and then spiking his drink, making him drowsy. She then contacted Morrigan Corde and informed the older woman of her success. However, Corde was not pleased that Gunn had told Gromia about Cade. Gunn replied that she was only following regulations by reporting to her senior officer; Corde countered that black ops did not apply. She warned Gunn that there was a leak on the Moff Council, and Gunn may become a target. Gunn, however, refused to believe her, stating that Corde was acting like her mother. On the way to [[Imperial Station Bravo]], Cade caused Gunn to crash her [[X-34 landspeeder/Legends|X-34 landspeeder]] by [[Telekinesis/Legends|Force choking]] her.

[[File:Family_Reunion.jpg|thumb|left|200px|Gunn Yage with her mother, Nyna Calixte (in her guise as Morrigan Corde), and her half-brother, Cade Skywalker.]]
Afterward, they were [[Mission to Tatooine (Gunn Yage)|attacked]] by the assassins and were forced to flee and collaborate to survive due to a sandstorm.<ref name="Legacy 38">[[Legacy (2006) 38|''Legacy'' (2006) 38]]</ref> Cade was going to leave Gunn to die, but the [[Force ghost/Legends|Force ghost]] of Luke Skywalker led them to the [[Lars homestead/Legends|Lars homestead]] to wait out the storm. There, Gunn insulted the [[New Jedi Order|Jedi Order]], only to be lectured by Cade. Gunn then told Skywalker that her father was the "Hero of Ossus." When Cade said that Rulf was a monster, Gunn revealed that her father never saw himself as a hero and was only following orders. After the massacre he was [[Alcohol/Legends|drunk]] for a [[Standard week/Legends|week]] and had never been the same. The revelation that Rulf and Gunn had softer sides placated Cade and Gunn went to sleep. She awoke to the [[Anzat (species)|Anzati]] assassins [[Sint Yoru|Sint]] and [[Nakia Yoru]] trying to steal her and Cade's "[[Soup (Anzati)|soup]]" and once again she counted on her half-brother for survival.<ref name="Legacy 39">[[Legacy (2006) 39|''Legacy'' (2006) 39]]</ref>

The siblings eventually defeated the Anzati pair, killing Sint in the process. They left the homestead to find Corde and Syn killed the [[Blood Carver]]. Corde reprimanded Yage for not waiting for her, and sent her to find evidence of Gromia's involvement in the attempted assassination.

Discreetly returning to Gromia's base, Gunn searched through her manifests and learns that items she had purchased were too expensive for a Moff's salary, proving that she was corrupt. During a confrontation with Gromia, Gunn learned the truth about her family ties with Cade Skywalker and Morrigan Corde; Gromia had looked in both Corde and Calixte's files in an attempt to ruin Calixte, and found that they were the same person and that Calixte was once [[Kol Skywalker]]'s wife and Cade's mother. Gunn refused to believe this, and in the scuffle Gromia was shot. She died telling Gunn to use the new information to ruin her mother.

===Principles on the line===
{{Quote|Follow your orders, Skulls. Take them out.|Captain Yage complies with Lady Rauder's orders|<ref name="Legacy 48">[[Legacy (2006) 48|''Legacy'' (2006) 48]]</ref>}}
[[File:Gunner_napdu.jpg|thumb|right|160px|Gunn Yage orders her squad to fire on unarmed civilian vessels]]
Skull Squadron was later selected to take part in [[Operation: Thunderstroke]], an effort ordered by the now-[[Regent/Legends||Regent]] Veed to capture Roan Fel, who was to meet with representatives of the Jedi Order on the planet [[Agamar/Legends|Agamar]]. En route to Agamar, Gunn learned that her squadron's TIE Predators were missing, and that they had been selected to pilot a [[Sith-Imperial starfighter|new type]] of starfighter and met the Sith overseer of the starfighter pilots: [[Darth Rauder]], who warned Gunn of the price for disobeying her orders.

As the battle between the Sith–Imperial and Fel loyalist forces raged on Agamar's orbit, Gunn was ordered by Lady Rauder to kill an ejected pilot loyal to Fel. Gunn refused to do so, since she saw that as murder, prompting Rauder to [[Torture/Legends|torture]] her using [[The Force/Legends|the Force]]. To spare Gunn from such a choice, "Crasher" Rimon took the shot and killed the helpless pilot, much to Rauder's anger. Gunn was clearly apalled, but kept on fighting. The battle ended with Roan Fel's escape, but the capture of [[Princess/Legends|Princess]] [[Marasiah Fel]].

Some days later, Skull Squadron also took part on the [[Devastation of Da Soocha|attack]] on [[Da Soocha]] and its moon [[Napdu]]. Da Soocha was poisoned in the same manner as [[Dac/Legends|Dac]] had been, while Napdu was subject to [[Orbital bombardment/Legends|orbital bombardment]]. A number of civilian vessels attempted to flee, but Lady Rauder classified them as enemy combatants and ordered their destruction. Despite the protests of both "Crasher" Rimon and "Storm" Akura, who confirmed the vessels to be unarmed, Gunn nevertheless ordered her pilots to target the escaping ships.

===Defection===
{{Quote|You raised me to act with honor. But there is no honor following the Sith.|Gunn to her father|<ref name="War 4">[[Legacy — War 4|''Legacy — War'' 4]]</ref>}}
[[File:DeathofRauder.jpg|thumb|left|150px|Gunn Yage shoots down Darth Rauder.]]
A few months later, in the [[138 ABY|final year]] of the [[Second Imperial Civil War]], Gunn Yage was at her father's side aboard the [[Star Destroyer/Legends|Star Destroyer]] ''[[War Hammer]]''. After keeping the secret of her mother's dual identity for so much time, she felt the need to inform her father that Nyna Calixte and Morrigan Corde were one and the same. However, Gunn was interrupted by a [[Holocomm/Legends|holocomm]] communication with Morlish Veed. The Grand Moff revealed that Yage's mother was a spy for Roan Fel, and thus he executed her for betraying the Empire.

Soon after, the Sith learned of a [[Hidden Temple (Sith–Imperial War)|secret Jedi Temple]] on [[Taivas]]. Assembling an armada of Sith and Imperial fighters, the Empire [[Attack on the Hidden Temple|attacked]] Taivas. After suffering initial friendly fire casualties due to Taivas' atmosphere, the Sith-Imperials sent in their ground forces. During this time, Yage and her squadron engaged in aerial combat with the Jedi. During the fight, the ''Mynock'' entered the fight and Yage was ordered to shoot it down. Torn between duty and personal sentiment, she was outraged that her Sith superiors had ordered her to kill her own half-brother.

As the Jedi and their allies held their ground, Darth Krayt ordered his forces to redouble their efforts. With an entire fleet of Krayt's new [[Dragon ship|capital ships]] and [[Annihilator-class starfighter|starfighters]], along with a legion of [[Darth Krayt's Sith trooper|newly-bred Sith troopers]], [[Darth Nihl]] launched a relentless assault on the Jedi's position. The new wave of attacks, however, resulted in a major case of friendly fire, thus resulting in the deaths of their own compatriots, including [[Moff/Legends|Moff]] [[Geist (Moff)|Geist]].

[[File:Yages_Defection.jpg|thumb|right|250px|Gunn Yage deserts one emperor for another.]]
As the battle raged on, [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] [[T'ra Saa/Legends|T'ra Saa]] ordered the ships that formed the Hidden Temple to take flight and escape. Aboard one of these ships, Masters [[Tili Qua]] and [[K'Kruhk]] took a number of Jedi [[Jedi Initiate/Legends|younglings]] to safety. Sensing their presence aboard the ship, Darth Rauder ordered Skull Squadron to destroy it. But Rauder's order turned out to be the final straw for Captain Gunn Yage, who could not bring herself to murder defenseless children. Hence, she came to the Jedi's defense by shooting down Rauder's starfighter, killing her Sith superior in act of repentance for her service to the Sith. Upon fully realizing that the Sith were evil and without honor, Yage denounced the Galactic Empire of Darth Krayt and re-avowed her former allegiance to Roan Fel. When she informed her father of the reasons behind her defection, Moff Yage followed his daughter's example and turned against the regime, having since grown to hate it for forcing him to commit many atrocities over the years. After killing the Sith who was in charge of the ''War Hammer'', Moff Yage also announced his defection and called on his fellow officers to join him.

Prior to the conflict's conclusion, Corde revealed herself to be alive to Gunn and Rulf. Both appeared to be pleased by the revelation of Corde's survival. The war ultimately came to an end in a [[Battle of Coruscant (Second Imperial Civil War)|final battle]] on [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]]. Gunn Yage's Jedi half-brother, Cade Skywalker was responsible for killing Darth Krayt, thus delivering the Galaxy from the Dark Lord's reign while the remnants of the One Sith dispersed into hiding.

==Personality and traits==
{{Quote|Family? We were ''never'' a family, "mother"—even ''before'' you took up with Veed. Dad… What did you ever see in such a nasty old ''rancor''?!|Gunn Yage|<ref name="Legacy 15" />}}
[[File:LovingFather-SWL14p15.jpg|thumb|left|200px|Gunn Yage was a product of her father's principles, such as honor and discipline.]]
As the daughter and only child of a career officer, Gunn Yage was born as a virtual military brat who was expected to follow in her father's footsteps. Under Moff Rulf Yage's supervision, Gunn was raised to believe in principles such as honor and duty, discipline and courage. Aside from her exceptional skills as a [[Imperial Navy/Legends|Imperial]] [[Pilot/Legends|pilot]], Yage was also a gifted leader who commanded the respect of her fellow pilots within [[1st Imperial Center Core Defense Squadron|Skull Squadron]]. Although she had been born as a girl to a father who wanted a boy, she was fiercely loyal to Moff Yage and persevered to prove herself as a worthy heir to their [[Yage|family name]].<ref name="Legacy 14" />

Despite Yage's close relationship to her father, she suffered from abandonment issues with her mother, Moff Nyna Calixte, who left the family and eventually became the lover of Grand Moff Morlish Veed. For years, Yage harbored feelings of contempt and resentment towards Calixte.<ref name="Legacy 15" /> She was initially devoted to serving Darth Krayt and the Galactic Empire, but inwardly she felt disdain for the Sith's brutal policies. Thus, she outwardly celebrated her father as the "hero of [[Ossus/Legends|Ossus]]," even though the [[Massacre at Ossus|massacre]] of the Jedi weighed heavily on his conscience.<ref name="Legacy 39" />

A steadfast Imperialist, she resented the members of the [[New Jedi Order|Jedi Order]] as enemies of the Empire. Upon discovering that she and Cade Skywalker were half-siblings who had the same mother, Yage was shocked almost to the point of total disbelief. Although the revelation did nothing to change her bitter resentment towards Calixte, she chose to not destroy her mother by revealing the details of Calixte's past to the other Moffs.<ref name="Legacy 39" />

Captain Yage's service to Darth Krayt's Galactic Empire came to an abrupt end when she realized that she could no longer serve the Sith without sacrificing her honor in the process. Though she knew next to nothing about her half-brother, Yage had no interest in murdering her half-brother; the thought of killing innocent children—even those raised and trained by the Jedi—was also an abhorrent crime in her eyes. In the end, Gunn Yage's moral code caused her to condemn the Sith as unethical and without honor.<ref name="War 4" />

==Behind the scenes==
{{Quote|It's difficult not to look to character types when designing new characters. No matter how hard you try to develop someone different, there is always some other character always comes to mind. I knew I was taking a risk with Gunner who does remind some folks of Kara on BSG by making her look like I did. I went through a lot of looks for her and remembered my niece always wore her hair that way and how great it looked on her. The look just seemed to suit the character better than any other take I had on her.|Jan Duursema|<ref name="Starbuck">{{JCFCite|url=posts/30637140|board=Authors and Artists|thread=The Official Jan Duursema Discussion Thread|user=[[Jan Duursema|Jan_Duursema]]|date=2009-09-29|time=6:22 AM|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20150907193625/https://boards.theforce.net/threads/the-official-jan-duursema-discussion-thread.5915607/page-95}}</ref>}}
When designing Yage's appearance, artist [[Jan Duursema]] considered many different looks before finding inspiration from a particular hairstyle worn by her niece. The look reminded her of [[Wikipedia:Kara Thrace|Kara Thrace]], aka "Starbuck", from [[Wikipedia:Battlestar Galactica (2004 TV series)|Battlestar Galactica]], and Duursema believed it was a risky move given the similarities between the characters. She chose it due to the look suiting the character, but fans of ''[[Star Wars: Legacy]]'' ultimately did make comparisons between Yage and Starbuck.<ref name="Starbuck" />

In the [[Legacy — War 6|sixth issue]] of [[Star Wars: Legacy — War|''Star Wars: Legacy—War'']], Yage's hair is drawn significantly longer than usual.
